extra.reasoning: Property: never (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). rec_drug_b can only be set true by r5 (priority 50, IF heart_failure). But r4 (priority 90, IF heart_failure THEN rec_drug_b := false) shares the exact same trigger (heart_failure) and has higher priority, so whenever r5 matches, r4 also matches and wins the per-output priority contest, forcing rec_drug_b := false. When heart_failure is false, neither r4 nor r5 fires and rec_drug_b falls to its default of false. Therefore rec_drug_b is false for every one of the 8 input combinations, making (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) unsatisfiable. rec_drug_a being true (via r1 when heart_failure) is irrelevant since rec_drug_b is never true. Full enumeration of all 8 (heart_failure, low_ejection_fraction, hepatic_impairment) combinations confirms rec_drug_b is always false. The property holds across the entire input space.
